Real (Effective, average) Power
$$
\frac{P=U·J}{P=|U||J|\underbrace{ \cos(\varphi) }_{ pf }} \ \ \ \ [W]
$$
Apparent Power
$S=|U||I| \ \ \ \ \ [V·A]$
Reactive Power
$Q=|U||I|\sin \varphi \ [VAR]$ 
VAR - reactive volt amp
Complex Power
$S(j\omega)=P+jQ=Se^{ j\varphi } \ [VA]$

---

What is the true power, in W consumed in a 10 V RMS series RLC circuit, given that its impedance is Z=10Ω and its resistance is R=6Ω?

$|Z|=10$
$|I|=\frac{|U|}{|Z|}=1A$

$P=|I|^{2}·R=6W$
True power can only be consumed by resistor

---

$|I|=\frac{3}{\sqrt{ 2 }}$
$-jx_{C}=-j 4$
$P=500W$
$P=\left( \frac{3}{\sqrt{ 2 }} \right)^{2}·R$
$R=111.25Ω$

---

An inductive load absorbs 220W of real power at a power factory of 0.8 lagging, and its terminal voltage is 240V RMS. Find the real power absorbed by the transmission line resistance of 5Ω, in W

$\cos \varphi=0.8$

$P=|U||I|pf$

$|I|=\frac{P}{|U|pf}=\frac{220}{240.08}=1.15A$

$P_{Line}=|I|^{2}·R_{ue}=(1.15)^{2}·5=6.61W$
